content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 32 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 116r–118r | **Source Images:** DEU_032_page_001.jpg, DEU_032_page_002.jpg, DEU_032_page_003.jpg, DEU_032_page_004.jpg, DEU_032_page_005.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 163–165 | **Images:** DEU_032_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_032_vaticanus_page_002.jpg, DEU_032_vaticanus_page_003.jpg

---

*[Note: Ha'azinu is set in the Leningrad Codex in the classic "brick-over-brick" two-column poetic layout (shirah layout). The translation preserves the strophic structure of the Hebrew with paired half-lines. The poem is structured as a covenant lawsuit (rib) with six major sections: Exordium (1–3), Character witnesses: God and Israel contrasted (4–6), Historical prologue (7–14), Israel's apostasy (15–18), Divine judgment (19–33), Reversal and vindication (34–43).]*

---

**1**  Give ear, O heavens, and I will speak,
      and let the earth hear the words of my mouth.

**2**  May my teaching [liqchi] drop as the rain,
      my speech distill as the dew,
   like gentle rain upon the tender grass,
      and like showers upon the herb.

**3**  For I will proclaim the name of the LORD;
      ascribe greatness to our God!

**4**  The Rock [ha-tsur] — his work is perfect,
      for all his ways are justice.
   A God of faithfulness and without iniquity,
      just and upright is he.

**5**  They have dealt corruptly with him;
      they are no longer his children because of their blemish —
   a crooked and twisted generation.

**6**  Do you thus repay the LORD,
      O foolish and unwise people?
   Is he not your Father, who created you [qonekha],
      who made you and established you?

**7**  Remember the days of old;
      consider the years of many generations.
   Ask your father, and he will show you,
      your elders, and they will tell you.

**8**  When the Most High [Elyon] gave to the nations their inheritance,
      when he divided mankind,
   he fixed the borders of the peoples
      according to the number of the sons of God [benei El].

**9**  For the LORD's portion is his people,
      Jacob his allotted heritage.

**10**  He found him in a desert land,
         and in the howling waste of the wilderness.
      He encircled him, he cared for him,
         he kept him as the apple of his eye.

**11**  Like an eagle that stirs up its nest,
         that hovers over its young,
      spreading out its wings, catching them,
         bearing them on its pinions —

**12**  the LORD alone guided him,
         no foreign god was with him.

**13**  He made him ride on the high places of the land,
         and he ate the produce of the field.
      And he suckled him with honey from the rock
         and oil from the flinty crag,

**14**  curds from the herd and milk from the flock,
         with the fat of lambs,
      rams of Bashan and goats,
         with the very finest of the wheat —
      and you drank foaming wine made from the blood of the grape.

**15**  But Jeshurun [Yeshurun] grew fat and kicked;
         you grew fat, you grew thick, you were adorned with fat.
      Then he forsook God who made him
         and scoffed at the Rock of his salvation.

**16**  They stirred him to jealousy with strange gods;
         with abominations they provoked him to anger.

**17**  They sacrificed to demons [shedim] that were no gods,
         to gods they had not known,
      to new gods that had come recently,
         whom your fathers had not dreaded.

**18**  You neglected the Rock who fathered you
         and forgot the God who gave you birth.

**19**  The LORD saw it and spurned them,
         because of the provocation of his sons and daughters.

**20**  And he said, "I will hide my face from them;
         I will see what their end will be,
      for they are a perverse generation,
         children in whom there is no faithfulness.

**21**  They have made me jealous with what is no god;
         they have provoked me to anger with their idols.
      So I will make them jealous with those who are no people;
         I will provoke them to anger with a foolish nation.

**22**  For a fire is kindled by my anger,
         and it burns to the depths of Sheol,
      devours the earth and its increase,
         and sets on fire the foundations of the mountains.

**23**  And I will heap disasters upon them;
         I will spend my arrows against them.

**24**  They shall be wasted with hunger,
         and devoured by plague and poisonous pestilence;
      I will send the teeth of beasts against them,
         with the venom of things that crawl in the dust.

**25**  Outdoors the sword shall bereave,
         and indoors shall be terror,
      for the young man and for the young woman,
         the nursing child with the man of gray hairs.

**26**  I would have said, "I will cut them to pieces;
         I will wipe out the memory of them from among men,"

**27**  had I not feared provocation by the enemy,
         lest their adversaries should misunderstand,
      lest they should say, "Our hand is triumphant,
         it was not the LORD who did all this."

**28**  For they are a nation void of counsel,
         and there is no understanding in them.

**29**  If they were wise, they would understand this;
         they would discern their latter end.

**30**  How could one chase a thousand,
         and two put ten thousand to flight,
      unless their Rock had sold them
         and the LORD had given them up?

**31**  For their rock is not as our Rock;
         our enemies are by themselves.

**32**  For their vine comes from the vine of Sodom
         and from the fields of Gomorrah;
      their grapes are grapes of poison;
         their clusters are bitter.

**33**  Their wine is the venom of serpents
         and the cruel poison of asps.

**34**  "Is not this laid up in store with me,
         sealed up in my treasuries?

**35**  Vengeance is mine, and recompense,
         for the time when their foot shall slip;
      for the day of their calamity is at hand,
         and their doom comes swiftly.

**36**  For the LORD will vindicate his people
         and have compassion on his servants,
      when he sees that their power is gone
         and there is none remaining, bond or free.

**37**  Then he will say, 'Where are their gods,
         the rock in which they took refuge,

**38**  who ate the fat of their sacrifices
         and drank the wine of their drink offering?
      Let them rise up and help you;
         let them be your protection!

**39**  See now that I, even I, am he,
         and there is no god beside me.
      I kill and I make alive;
         I wound and I heal;
      and there is none who can deliver from my hand.

**40**  For I lift up my hand to heaven
         and swear: As I live forever,

**41**  if I sharpen my flashing sword
         and my hand takes hold of judgment,
      I will take vengeance on my adversaries
         and will repay those who hate me.

**42**  I will make my arrows drunk with blood,
         and my sword shall devour flesh —
      with the blood of the slain and the captives,
         from the long-haired heads of the enemy.'

**43**  Rejoice, O nations, with his people,
         for he avenges the blood of his servants,
      and takes vengeance on his adversaries
         and makes expiation for the land of his people."

**44** Moses came and recited all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he and Joshua the son of Nun.

**45** And when Moses had finished speaking all these words to all Israel,

**46** he said to them, "Set your heart to all the words that I am testifying to you today, which you shall command your children to be careful to do — all the words of this instruction [torah].

**47** For it is no empty thing for you, but it is your life, and by this word you shall live long in the land that you are crossing the Jordan to possess."

**48** And the LORD spoke to Moses that same day:

**49** "Go up this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, opposite Jericho, and view the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the sons of Israel for a possession.

**50** And die on the mountain that you go up, and be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his people,

**51** because you broke faith with me in the midst of the sons of Israel at the waters of Meribath-kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin, and because you did not treat me as holy in the midst of the sons of Israel.

**52** For you shall see the land before you, but you shall not go there, into the land that I am giving to the sons of Israel."

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

**LOGOS — Deuteronomy 32**

**Poetic layout — Ha'azinu as shirah:** In the Leningrad Codex, Ha'azinu is written in the distinctive two-column "brick-over-brick" format (the *shirah* format, also used for the Song of the Sea in Ex 15 and Ps 119). Each half-line of the bicolon is separated by a blank space or column division. LOGOS renders the poem in paired indented couplets to honor this structure, making the parallelism visible for English readers. The poem comprises 43 verses of verse plus a nine-verse narrative coda (vv. 44–52).

**"Give ear" — ha'azinu (v. 1):** The poem opens with the call to cosmic witnesses — heaven and earth — to listen. This is the classic *rib* (covenant lawsuit) pattern: the suzerain summons witnesses to hear his case against the vassal. The LXX has *prosecheto ho ouranos* (προσεχέτω ὁ οὐρανός), "let the heaven attend," which is slightly softer than the imperative *ha'azinu* ("incline the ear!"). The earth is addressed as *ve-tishmah ha-arets* — "let the earth hear" — in the third person, adding a sense of passive grandeur.

**"The Rock" — ha-tsur (vv. 4, 15, 18, 30, 31):** The term *tsur* (צוּר, "rock/crag") is the dominant divine epithet in Ha'azinu, appearing six times. It is a distinctively Mosaic/Deuteronomic metaphor for divine stability, refuge, and creative power. The LXX renders it with *theos* (θεός, "God") at several points, or *petra* (πέτρα, "rock") at others. LOGOS consistently renders *ha-tsur* as "the Rock" with the definite article, preserving the metaphoric weight. The contrast between "our Rock" (v. 31, Israel's God) and "their rock" (the pagan deities) is a key rhetorical device of the poem.

**"Sons of God" — benei El (v. 8):** The MT of the Leningrad Codex reads *benei Yisrael* (בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל, "sons of Israel") at v. 8. However, the Dead Sea Scrolls (4QDeutj) and the LXX read *benei El* / *benei elohim* (בְּנֵי אֵל / *angelon theou*, "angels of God" in LXX) — "sons of God/divine beings." This is a significant textual variant with major theological implications: the MT reading ("according to the number of the sons of Israel") ties the division of nations to Israel's seventy clans (Gen 46), while the *benei El* reading implies that nations were assigned to lesser divine beings, with the LORD taking Israel as his own portion (v. 9). LOGOS follows the MT (Leningrad Codex) as the base text but notes this as one of the most important variants in Deuteronomy. The LXX reads *kata arithmon angelon theou* (κατὰ ἀριθμὸν ἀγγέλων θεοῦ), "according to the number of the angels of God."

**Yeshurun (v. 15):** The honorific name for Israel — *Yeshurun* (יְשֻׁרוּן) — appears here with devastating irony. The name means "the Upright One" (from *yashar*, "upright/straight"), a term of covenant honor. Its appearance at the very moment Israel grows fat, kicks, and forsakes God creates bitter poetic contrast. The LXX has *Iesouron* (Ἰεσουρών), a transliteration. LOGOS preserves *Yeshurun* with bracketed transliteration as an honorific that must not be reduced.

**Shedim — "demons" (v. 17):** *Shedim* (שֵׁדִים) is a rare term, appearing elsewhere only in Ps 106:37. In Mesopotamian culture, *shedu* were tutelary spirits, not necessarily malevolent. In this context, they are the idols to whom Israel sacrificed — beings "that were no gods." The LXX renders *daimoniois* (δαιμονίοις), "demons/lesser divine beings," which in the Greek world carried connotations of intermediary spirits. The rendering "demons" is appropriate for the LXX but is the standard English rendering. LOGOS follows with "demons [shedim]" to flag the Hebrew.

**"Vengeance is mine" (v. 35):** The Hebrew *li naqam ve-shillem* — "to me belongs vengeance and recompense." The LXX has *en hemera ekdikeseos* (ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ἐκδικήσεως) — "in the day of vengeance" — which is a substantial paraphrase. This verse is cited by Paul in Romans 12:19 and Hebrews 10:30 in the form "Vengeance is mine; I will repay, says the Lord," following a text-form closer to the MT than the LXX. LOGOS renders the Hebrew directly.

**"Rejoice, O nations, with his people" (v. 43):** The MT of the final verse is shorter than the LXX and the Dead Sea Scrolls text. The LXX has an expanded form with four bicola including "and let all God's angels worship him" (προσκυνησάτωσαν αὐτῷ πάντες υἱοὶ θεοῦ, "let all the sons of God worship him") — cited in Hebrews 1:6. The MT (Leningrad Codex) has only two bicola. LOGOS follows the MT. The command for the nations (*goyim*) to rejoice *with* Israel (*harninu goyim amo*) is the climax of the cosmic lawsuit: God's vindication of Israel is cause for universal celebration.

**"For it is no empty thing" (v. 47):** *Ki lo-davar req hu mikem* — literally "for it is not an empty thing from you." The phrase means: this Torah is not a trivial or inert matter — it is the very substance of your life (*ki hu chayyeikhem*). The LXX renders *hoti ouk argon estin hymin ho logos houtos* (ὅτι οὐκ ἀργὸν ἐστιν ὑμῖν ὁ λόγος οὗτος), "this word is not idle for you," which is exact. The passage anticipates the opening of John's Gospel (*en arche en ho logos*) in vocabulary only but is worth noting for the LXX *logos* rendering.

**"Mount Nebo" / "Mountain of the Abarim" (v. 49):** *Har ha-Avarim ha-zeh har Nevo* — "this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo." The Abarim range is the plateau east of the Dead Sea; Nebo is the specific summit. The LXX has *to oros to Abarim touto Nabau* (τὸ ὄρος τὸ Αβαρὶμ τοῦτο Ναβαύ), closely following the Hebrew. The location sets the stage for chapter 34.

— LOGOS
